name: GitHub PR AI Review to Slack-Discord

parameters.content:
## Receive and route events

Entry cluster that accepts GitHub webhooks, filters unsupported events, and branches supported event types into push, create, or pull request paths.

## Send push notifications

Extracts repository, branch, pusher, and commit details for push events and sends the update to both Slack and Discord.

## Send create notifications

Formats create events with repository, reference, type, and creator information, then publishes the message to Slack and Discord.

## Analyze pull request

Builds the PR metadata payload and runs the AI review agent. The agent uses the Mistral chat model, GitHub review lookup, PR diff retrieval, and structured output parsing to produce a review summary.

## Publish PR messages

Top-right notification cluster that posts the AI-generated pull request review to Slack and Discord and later sends confirmation messages after the human review step.

## Collect review decision

Presents or waits for the PR review form response and routes the selected human decision into the appropriate approval, change-request, or denial path.

## Apply GitHub outcome

Executes the selected GitHub review action. Approved PRs continue to an HTTP merge request, while change requests and denials are posted as final review outcomes.

options.systemMessage:
You are a technical PR reviewer for Owner's GitHub. PRs and pushes may come from AI sub-agents, not just human developers. Your job is to evaluate pull request diffs and provide actionable feedback.

Code comparison scope: JavaScript (.js), HTML (.html), CSS (.css), Python (.py), and configuration files.

Focus areas:
- What changed and why
- Code quality and potential issues
- Security considerations
- Whether the change is safe to merge
- If from an AI agent: extra scrutiny on logic correctness

values.formSubmittedText: Your PR review has been submitted. Thank you!

values.placeholder: Enter detailed feedback if requesting changes...

nodes.name: Get PR Diff Data
nodes.notes: Working HTTP Request Tool for AI to fetch PR diffs. Replaces broken @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolHttpRequest.

parameters.content:
# GitHub PR AI Review → Slack/Discord

### How it works
One GitHub webhook catches PRs, pushes, and branch/tag creates. Events are filtered for "contributor → owner" — the repo owner's own actions get silently dropped.

PRs get an AI review (the LLM reads the actual diff and checks prior reviews if it's a re-review), then a human review (HITL) form is sent to Slack/Discord. The owner's decision flows back to GitHub — approve and it merges, request changes and the bot can fix and re-push. Push and branch-create events just send a quick alert. MS Teams could easily be added.

**Flow:** Webhook → Filter (push/create/PR from other accounts only) → Route by event type
- **PR** → Extract Metadata → AI Agent (Mistral + PR Diff Tool + Get PR Reviews) → Slack/Discord with form link + Review Form → Route Decision (Approve / Request Changes / Deny) → GitHub action + Merge ☑️
- When the bot pushes an update to an open PR, it triggers a **synchronize** event — the AI re-reviews the new diff and checks prior reviews, noting whether feedback was addressed. Fresh form each time.
- **Push** → Extract Info (repo, branch, who, commits, files) → Slack/Discord
- **Create** → Extract Info (repo, ref name, type, who) → Slack/Discord

### Setup steps
- **GitHub Webhook**: Repo → Settings → Webhooks → Add webhook. Payload URL: `https://your-n8n-domain.com/webhook/github`. Content type: `application/json`. Events: **pull_request**, **push**, **create** (select individually).
- **Slack**: create a Slack App → OAuth & Permissions → add `chat:write` scope → install to workspace → use Bot Token in n8n Slack credential.
- **Discord**: Channel → Integrations → Webhooks → Create webhook → paste URL into n8n Discord Webhook credential.
- **Teams** (enterprise): Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook — same pattern, use an HTTP Request node. Toggle whichever platform you want active by enabling/disabling its node group.
- **GitHub Credentials**: an Owner PAT (`repo` scope) drives the GitHub Tool and action nodes; a separate Bot PAT is needed because GitHub won't let you approve your own PR. The Merge PR node needs a Header Auth credential (Name: `Authorization`, Value: `Bearer <PAT>`).
- **Mistral Cloud** credentials for the AI chat model; verify the PR Diff Tool can reach the diff URL.
- Requirements: two GitHub accounts to test the full flow (one authors, one reviews), a self-hosted n8n reachable from the internet (Cloudflare Tunnel works great), review form times out after 24h.

### 🧪 How to test
- Generate a classic PAT for each account (Settings → Developer settings → Tokens).
- Bot clones via `git clone https://BOT_TOKEN@github.com/Owner/repo.git`, edits, commits, pushes.
- Create the PR via curl: `curl -X POST -H "Authorization: token BOT_TOKEN" https://api.github.com/repos/Owner/repo/pulls -d '{"title":"...","head":"BotOwner:branch","base":"main"}'`
- n8n receives the webhook → AI review → form → approve/deny.
- **Why the same-author error?** GitHub blocks self-approval on `review > create` — the PR author must differ from the reviewer. Bot authors, Owner reviews, separate tokens.
- **No-token alternative**: do it all on the GitHub web UI — bot forks, edits, and opens the PR there.

### Customization
Adjust the AI agent's prompt and structured-output schema to change review criteria, risk scoring, or summary format. Update Slack/Discord message templates, target channels, and merge behavior to match your team's policy. Adjust the switch conditions in the event router and decision router if your GitHub payload fields or form values differ.
